original_hypothesis.title: Permanent Fund Lock via Arbitrary Refund Deadline Extension
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The `ILOManager` owner can call `setRefundDeadlineForProject` to set the `refundDeadline` to `type(uint64).max`. If a pool fails to launch (e.g., due to price manipulation), investors can never trigger a refund, permanently locking their funds.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This represents a severe trust model violation where a compromised or malicious protocol admin can hold all investor funds hostage indefinitely.
deployment_assumptions: Admin maliciously or accidentally sets refund deadline to type(uint64).max
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No deployment evidence, but the setter lacks bounds checking.

proof_of_signal.pattern: function setRefundDeadlineForProject(...) external override onlyOwner() { _project.refundDeadline = refundDeadline; }

preconditions: ILOManager owner is compromised or malicious

economic_questions: Can the owner be forced to change it back?
validation_requirements: Test setting deadline to max and attempting refund

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: After funds are raised but before launch
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Complete loss of investor trust and funds

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All investors in the targeted project
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Total raised amount for the project

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
